mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 5.0%, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in > 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0, or the signal's directional accuracy drops below 52% over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous rise in industrial production and housing starts signals broad-based economic acceleration, which expands profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC > 0.05, or SPX 60-day returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when both INDPRO and HOUST rise concurrently.

mechanism_narrative: In a rising rate environment, persistent core inflation above 2.5% forces the Fed to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances despite high inflation and rising rates, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth exceeding the historical 2.0% annualized trend indicates robust aggregate demand, which supports corporate revenue growth and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 50% over any 12-month rolling window, or GDPC1 growth decelerations consistently precede positive 20-day SPX returns.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls exceeding 200,000 signals strong labor demand, which reduces recession risk premiums and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of months where PAYEMS exceeds the 200k threshold, or the signal's predictive power vanishes when controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales combined with a flattening or declining federal funds rate indicates accommodative monetary policy supporting consumer spending, which expands equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term fails to explain > 5% of variance in 60-day forward returns, or SPX 60-day returns are negative when RSAFS rises and FEDFUNDS simultaneously declines.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of supply chain stress, a divergence where industrial production falls while unemployment rises signals stagflationary dynamics, which compresses equity multiples and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls > 0.5% MoM while UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the divergence signal's Rank IC remains below 0.02 over a 3-year out-of-sample test.
